result.raw_response: {"subjective": "Patient is a 52-year-old male presenting for annual wellness exam. Reports afternoon fatigue for approximately 2–3 months, occurring around 2–3 PM. No chest pain or shortness of breath. Sleeping approximately 7 hours per night; partner reports snoring. Notes unintentional weight loss of approximately 5 pounds over several months. Appetite intact.", "objective": "Vitals: BP 138/86 mmHg, HR 72 bpm (regular), Temp 98.4°F, O2 sat 98% on room air. Height 5'11\", weight 201 lbs, BMI 28. Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally. Heart: regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. Abdomen soft, non-tender. Labs: fasting glucose 112 mg/dL (elevated), total cholesterol 214 mg/dL, LDL 138 mg/dL, HDL 42 mg/dL, triglycerides 189 mg/dL, TSH 2.1 (WNL). CBC and CMP otherwise unremarkable.", "assessment": "1. Pre-diabetes (fasting glucose 112 mg/dL). 2. Borderline hyperlipidemia (LDL 138, low HDL 42, triglycerides 189). 3. Afternoon fatigue and unintentional weight loss — etiology unclear, requires monitoring.", "plan": "1. Start metformin 500 mg twice daily with meals for pre-diabetes management. 2. Start atorvastatin 20 mg once daily at bedtime for hyperlipidemia. 3. Dietary counseling: reduce refined carbohydrate intake; increase physical activity to at least 30 minutes of moderate exercise 5 days/week. 4. Refer to dietitian. 5. Recheck labs in 3 months. 6. Follow up in 3 months; return sooner if fatigue worsens or further unintentional weight loss occurs.", "medications": ["Metformin 500 mg oral twice daily with meals", "Atorvastatin 20 mg oral once daily at bedtime"]}

parsed.subjective: Patient is a 52-year-old male presenting for annual wellness exam. Reports afternoon fatigue for approximately 2–3 months, occurring around 2–3 PM. No chest pain or shortness of breath. Sleeping approximately 7 hours per night; partner reports snoring. Notes unintentional weight loss of approximately 5 pounds over several months. Appetite intact.
parsed.objective: Vitals: BP 138/86 mmHg, HR 72 bpm (regular), Temp 98.4°F, O2 sat 98% on room air. Height 5'11", weight 201 lbs, BMI 28. Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally. Heart: regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. Abdomen soft, non-tender. Labs: fasting glucose 112 mg/dL (elevated), total cholesterol 214 mg/dL, LDL 138 mg/dL, HDL 42 mg/dL, triglycerides 189 mg/dL, TSH 2.1 (WNL). CBC and CMP otherwise unremarkable.
parsed.assessment: 1. Pre-diabetes (fasting glucose 112 mg/dL). 2. Borderline hyperlipidemia (LDL 138, low HDL 42, triglycerides 189). 3. Afternoon fatigue and unintentional weight loss — etiology unclear, requires monitoring.
parsed.plan: 1. Start metformin 500 mg twice daily with meals for pre-diabetes management. 2. Start atorvastatin 20 mg once daily at bedtime for hyperlipidemia. 3. Dietary counseling: reduce refined carbohydrate intake; increase physical activity to at least 30 minutes of moderate exercise 5 days/week. 4. Refer to dietitian. 5. Recheck labs in 3 months. 6. Follow up in 3 months; return sooner if fatigue worsens or further unintentional weight loss occurs.
medications: Metformin 500 mg oral twice daily with meals
Atorvastatin 20 mg oral once daily at bedtime

tester_notes: Clean output. All four SOAP sections populated correctly. Medications correctly extracted with dose, route, frequency. Clinical language is professional. PASS.
